title: Fix the Recursive Trie Node Inserter

statement.markdown:
Write a recursive function to insert a word into a trie data structure. The function should handle the case where the word already exists in the trie and return the existing node without creating a new one. The trie is represented as a nested object where each key is a letter and the value is the next node or null if it's a leaf node. For example, if we insert the word 'apple', the trie should look like this:

```
{ 'a': { 'p': { 'p': { 'l': { 'e': null } } } } }
```
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the word to be inserted into the trie.
statement.outputFormat: The updated trie node after inserting the word.
statement.notes: The function should return the updated trie node. The trie node is an object where each key is a letter and the value is the next node or null if it's a leaf node.

constraints.description: The number of characters in the input word.

examples.explanation: Inserting the word 'apple' into an empty trie.
Inserting the word 'apple' into a trie that already contains it.
Inserting the word 'app' into a trie.

hints.text: First hint text: Start by checking if the current node has a key equal to the word's current character. If it does, return the current node.

editorial.title: Recursive Trie Node Inserter Editorial
editorial.intuition: The function should recursively traverse the trie and insert the word's characters into the trie. If the word already exists in the trie, return the existing node without creating a new one.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would involve iterating over the word's characters and inserting them into the trie one by one. This would result in a time complexity of O(n^2) where n is the number of characters in the word.
editorial.betterSolution: A better approach would involve using a recursive function to traverse the trie and insert the word's characters into the trie. This would result in a time complexity of O(n) where n is the number of characters in the word.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves using a recursive function to traverse the trie and insert the word's characters into the trie. If the word already exists in the trie, return the existing node without creating a new one. This results in a time complexity of O(n) where n is the number of characters in the word.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves showing that the function returns the updated trie node after inserting the word. The function recursively traverses the trie and inserts the word's characters into the trie. If the word already exists in the trie, return the existing node without creating a new one.
commonMistakes: Not handling the case where the word already exists in the trie.
Not returning the updated trie node.
